Empires & Puzzles Saskia Review: The 3-Star Dodge & Heal Prodigy

Is Saskia the best 3-star healer in Empires & Puzzles? We analyze her Fast mana, unique Enhanced Dodge mechanic, and how she counters the meta. Discover why this Woodland Faun is a tournament essential.
Saskia: The 3-Star Healer Who Dances Through Danger
Saskia, The Fleet-Footed Faun: Dancing Past Danger
Saskia is a Rare (3-star) Holy hero from the
family, originally released in August 2024. In a rarity tier often dominated by slow sluggers or fragile glass cannons, Saskia bounds in as a

speed healer with a defensive twist that can make hitting her team feel like trying to catch a greased piglet. While she doesn't have a costume yet, her base kit is complex enough to keep opponents guessing. She brings a fresh "dodge-then-heal" mechanic that punishes enemies for trying to dispel her.
The artwork features Saskia as a joyous faun carrying a deer (or maybe she

the deer's ride?), capturing a whimsical, forest-frolic vibe. It’s a refreshing break from the usual "gritting teeth and holding a glowing weapon" aesthetic. The colors are warm and vibrant, matching her Holy element. It’s basically what happens when Bambi decides to join the Avengers as a combat medic.
As of early 2026, Saskia's stats show a bit of age compared to the absolute bleeding edge of power creep (like the Construct or Shadow heroes released in late 2025). Her power of
is roughly 30-40 points behind the newest 3-stars (who sit around 530-540). However, her stat distribution is quite balanced. Her Attack (
) is respectable for a healer, allowing her to contribute some tile damage, while her Defense (
) are serviceable but will benefit significantly from Limit Breaking and Emblems to survive against modern snipers.
"Rejuvenating Leap" is a multi-layered skill that provides incredible value for
26% is a solid chunk of health, especially at Fast speed. It’s enough to undo a slash attack or a light special skill hit.

This is the chef's kiss. The dodge lasts until the hero dodges
. But when that dodge ends (either by dodging, being dispelled, or overwritten), they get a massive

It creates a win-win scenario. If the enemy hits you, you might dodge (0 damage). If you dodge, you lose the dodge buff but gain a massive regeneration buff. If the enemy tries to dispel the dodge, they trigger the regeneration. It forces the opponent to choose between missing attacks or dealing with a team that is constantly healing.

A 45% dodge chance is nearly a coin flip. In a 3-star tournament where dispels can be scarce or costly (using a hero slot), this can single-handedly waste an enemy's entire turn.

Upon release, Saskia was immediately recognized as a top-tier 3-star healer, rivaling
. She offered a faster alternative to the slow/average healers dominating the scene.
While raw stats have crept up by about 8-10% since her release, her
have not been crept. A 45% dodge at Fast speed is still premium utility.

Saskia remains a staple in 3-star tournaments. She is particularly dominant in Buff Booster (due to the buffs she provides) and Rush Attack (though her Fast speed is less of an advantage there, her passives shine). She is a "must-level" for any serious tournament player.
Saskia does not currently have a costume.

She provides buffs and heals fast.
Her family bonus protects her mana, and she heals effectively.
Her fast heal keeps the team topped up during stage progression.
Can anchor a 3-star clean-up team against weakened 4/5-star teams by dodging snipers.
Saskia works best when she can protect fragile hitters or stall for time.
Double heal, protection against Dark (Rekhetre), Dodge (Saskia), and dispel/block (Joukahainen). Very frustrating to kill.
Dawn controls mana, Kvasir blocks enemy minions, and Saskia keeps everyone alive and blinds any enemy minions that do spawn.
Bertulf boosts attack, Saskia keeps them alive, Poppy snipes. Fast and deadly.
While dispelling triggers her HoT, it removes the Dodge, leaving her team vulnerable to immediate heavy damage.
If dodge cannot be applied, she loses most of her utility.
Don't pair her with other dodge heroes if their dodge chance is higher, as they might overwrite each other inefficiently.
Saskia is a gem for new players. A Fast healer is a luxury in the early game. Prioritize leveling her as your main Yellow 3-star healer. She will carry you through map stages and early raid tiers.

You might overlook 3-stars, but Saskia is worth the limit break materials for tournament performance. Her specific immunity to Defense Down and Mana Control (with family bonus) allows her to counter specific meta threats in the 3-star arena that usually plague older heroes. She is a safe investment for your tournament roster.
When Saskia was released, the community immediately flagged her as a "must-have."
"Is Saskia worth Summoning For? Yes, absolutely."
— This was the overwhelming sentiment in poll results.
Players praised the synergy of her skills:
"As soon as you use your ability once, the game is on your side... The abilities are in sync perfectly."
"I have double limit broken Saskia. She is very very impressive! I highly recommend her as one of the very best 3
The comparison to 5-star mechanics was inevitable, with players noting that getting a dodge mechanic at 3-star Fast speed felt almost unfair.

Saskia is a playful reminder that you don't need 5 stars to be a superstar. She packs a complex, modern kit into a tiny, hoofed package. Her ability to dance around damage while providing substantial healing makes her one of the premier support heroes in her rarity. While her stats aren't quite "late 2025" level, her utility is timeless.
If you play 3-star tournaments, you need Saskia. She's not just a healer; she's a survival specialist.
Note: The Woodland Faun family bonus was adjusted post-release to be even stronger against mana reduction, cementing her place in the anti-meta.
